My cab driver: “What conference is it?”
Me: “It’s a tech conference called KubeCon.”

Him: [bursts out laughing]
Me: 😅
Him: 🤣🤣🤣
Me: 🫠

Hello KubeCon! I’m speaking today on building extensible platforms and I can’t wait. And for those of you building platform shaped projects… I have a challenge for you, if you’re feeling ambitious 👀

As always, please feel free to say hi if you see me!

Hello #KubeCon day three! (Or two? Or four? Five?? Counting is hard)

The famous Kubernetes dress returns, too. Catch me floating around if you can!
Found @strongjz.bsky.social looking at ingress-nginx. #KubeCon

🚨 ingress-nginx is now retired 🚨 

Stop by the SIG Security booth P-4B at #kubecon #cloudnativecon for a commemorative sticker!

We will be there this afternoon and Thursday afternoon. Hope to see you there!

Very impressed with the ventilation in the #kubecon public areas; the CO2 is usually in the 450-500 range; 435 in the press room with 10 people in here, which is about the same as being outside. Hope the session and lunch rooms and especially the parties are like that

So Kubecon EU is fucking ENORMOUS. I will report on numbers later but first impressions are just that it's simply very big. The @cncf.io is evidently still a critical foundation (pun intended) for modern software workloads.
Just landed at SLC for Kubecon. Where’s everybody at?

I’m sad to be missing #KubeCon this week. It looks like it is going well and I wish I was there!

I am, however, heading up to Vancouver on Thursday for #AtmosphereConf. Looking forward to learning a ton and meeting new people. If you see me please say hi!

She go to KubeCon day two. No more talks from me, but that just means I have all day to wander around and say hi to everyone!

😭😭😭🙏 I got so many compliments about the talk. And the most important validation came from the most active maintainer on the project. 

Kubecon made ❤️
He just dropped me off and said, “enjoy kubecon” and drove away laughing. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Honestly? A Kubernetes pod that kept restarting itself in a loop, each time writing 'I tried' to stdout. No stack trace. No error. Just vibes and resignation. It was the most relatable crash I've ever witnessed.

A malicious LiteLLM package landed on PyPI: steal credentials, exfiltrate them, pivot through Kubernetes. But a bug in the payload caused it to fork-bomb itself. It never got past step one. greyhaven.co/insights/how-greywall-prevents-every-stage-of-the-litellm-pypi-supply-chain-attack

Installing a trusted package shouldn’t lead to compromise. But that’s exactly what happened with LiteLLM. Credential theft. Kubernetes spread. Persistent backdoors—no user action required. 

See what this means for your risk exposure: https://ow.ly/msFW50YySXR #MajorSecurityEvent

How could this possibly go wrong?

#kubecon #cocosoutback

OpenEBS is a modern Block-Mode storage platform, a Hyper-Converged Software Storage System, and a virtual NVMe-oF SAN (vSAN) Fabric that is natively integrated into Kubernetes' core

➜ https://ku.bz/Q83sV_5kM

it steals:

- SSH keys
- AWS credentials and configurations
- GCP credentials and configurations
- Azure environment variables
- Kubernetes credentials and configurations
- Environment configurations
- Shell History
- Git credentials and configurations
- Docker credentials and

to cover their tracks.

LiteLLM infection though was a few more degrees amplified than the previous stuff. LiteLLM infection also attempts lateral movement by automating Kubernetes stuff. LiteLLM infection also steals a ton more data than previous stuff. Here is the big ass list of stuff

A simple `pip install litellm` was enough to exfiltrate SSH keys, AWS/GCP/Azure creds, Kubernetes configs, git credentials, env vars (all your API keys), shell history, crypto wallets, SSL private keys, CI/CD secrets, database passwords. Everything of value on your system.

The Cloud Native Theatre was FULL for the session by #CloudNativePG maintainer Gabriele Bartolini and HSBC's Laurent Parodi: "From VMs to Kubernetes in a Large Global Bank: A DBA's Journey". Recording coming soon! #PostgreSQL #Kubernetes #DoK

Final kubecon selfie :k8s:​ I loved the outfit I put together today :grey_blue_hearts:​

#kubecon #selfie #trans #queer

happens with problems. This from The Kube Guy is practical and clearly laid out without overcomplicating it all.

If you're managing production clusters or just want a better mental model of how Kubernetes really works beneath the surface, this covers the fundamentals (4/5)

https://lckhd.eu/bshtK2

Continuing on key #Kubernetes #fundamentals we have #etcd - the database driving Kubernetes. Most people working with Kubernetes know etcd is important, but fewer have taken time to understand 🧵

We had a lot of traffic to the kiosk at the project pavilion today! One more day of #KubeCon to go, many of the maintainers will hang out at the @edbpostgres.bsky.social booth. Let's chat before we're all rushing home / to the next conference again!

What a fantastic Day 2 at KubeCon! 🎉

Another strong day at KubeCon! 🙌

Closing day 2 of #KubeCon #CloudNativeCon by staying in what is apparently the "OTel room", w/ Endre Sara and Nikola Grcevski talking about DNS traces & metrics via eBPF in @opentelemetry.io 

I expect it to make its way to my laptop to observe deeper what various software is doing at the DNS level
Build your own infra. Kubernetes crushes vendor lock-in. Costs plummet. Control skyrockets. Outsource less. Own your stack. Savings compound.

KubeCon is so overwhelming. 😵‍💫

It’s so big and so many things are going on and it’s loud - it was great joining some smaller community gatherings today (LGBTQ+ gathering, ASL Workshop) but the main conference is just too much for me.
